title: Thread Creation Pathways in Java
description: A flowchart illustrating the two primary methods for creating and starting a thread in Java: extending the Thread class and implementing the Runnable interface.
relationships: Main Program can choose to create a class that 'extends Thread' OR 'implements Runnable'
Both pathways require overriding the 'run()' method with the thread's task
An instance of the class is created (a 'Thread Object' if extending, or passed to a Thread constructor if implementing)
The 'start()' method is called on the Thread object, which causes the JVM to call the 'run()' method in a new thread of execution
